raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The contribution claim ('We introduce VariantTalk') is embedded mid-section after a results paragraph with no gap statement or motivation; the conceptual framing of what problem this solves and why it is needed is absent or displaced.","R1.2":"The opening paragraph asserts F1 scores are 'broadly comparable' to prior work and then immediately undermines that claim with a normalization caveat ('arguably somewhat complicated … approximated as faithfully as the published descriptions appeared to permit'); these two moves are logically in tension and the chain of reasoning collapses under scrutiny.","R1.3":"No F1 scores are actually presented in the excerpt; the comparability assertion is made without the underlying numbers, making evidence-claim alignment unverifiable from the text as written.","R1.5":"Results (paragraph 1) precede the system description (paragraph 2) and the methodology (paragraphs 3–4), inverting the conventional structure; this ordering forces a reader to evaluate a claim before the system being evaluated has been defined.","R1.6":"The opening sentence is a single run-on clause stacked with hedges ('we believe to be broadly comparable,' 'arguably somewhat complicated,' 'appeared to permit'); the cumulative hedging weakens the scientific voice and impedes clarity throughout the paragraph."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should this review prioritize scientific and technical integrity—specifically whether the evidence supports the claims and whether the reasoning is sound—or writing clarity and prose quality, with the other concern treated as secondary?"}

rationale.R1.1: The contribution claim ('We introduce VariantTalk') is embedded mid-section after a results paragraph with no gap statement or motivation; the conceptual framing of what problem this solves and why it is needed is absent or displaced.
rationale.R1.2: The opening paragraph asserts F1 scores are 'broadly comparable' to prior work and then immediately undermines that claim with a normalization caveat ('arguably somewhat complicated … approximated as faithfully as the published descriptions appeared to permit'); these two moves are logically in tension and the chain of reasoning collapses under scrutiny.
rationale.R1.3: No F1 scores are actually presented in the excerpt; the comparability assertion is made without the underlying numbers, making evidence-claim alignment unverifiable from the text as written.
rationale.R1.5: Results (paragraph 1) precede the system description (paragraph 2) and the methodology (paragraphs 3–4), inverting the conventional structure; this ordering forces a reader to evaluate a claim before the system being evaluated has been defined.
rationale.R1.6: The opening sentence is a single run-on clause stacked with hedges ('we believe to be broadly comparable,' 'arguably somewhat complicated,' 'appeared to permit'); the cumulative hedging weakens the scientific voice and impedes clarity throughout the paragraph.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize scientific and technical integrity—specifically whether the evidence supports the claims and whether the reasoning is sound—or writing clarity and prose quality, with the other concern treated as secondary?
